claims.description: pushNowDeepLink calls setActiveScope({kind:'supporter-hub'}) before router.push whenever the route being pushed is support.hub, via a new optional setActiveScope callback on PushNowDeepLinkOptions.
LearnerMentorScreen threads setActiveScope from useScopeContext() into all three pushNowDeepLink call sites (card continue, bar-intent jump, error-cache resume fallback).
Spy-ordering test proves setActiveScope({kind:'supporter-hub'}) fires strictly before router.push('/(app)/mentor') for a support.hub-linked NowCard at the mentor.tsx caller.
JournalMomentsStrip threads setActiveScope from useScopeContext() into its pushNowDeepLink call.
Spy-ordering test against the real JournalMomentsStrip proves setActiveScope fires before router.push for a support.hub-linked ledger moment at the second caller.
Direct unit test proves pushNowDeepLink calls setActiveScope before router.push for a support.hub route, and never calls setActiveScope for a non-support.hub route.
Tests against the real ScopeContextProvider (not a mock) prove a support.hub pointer resolves to supporter-hub scope without throwing from Me scope and from a person scope.

[rework, WI-2223 rejection fix] Genuine stale-edge test: a persisted SecureStore scope key for a person scope predates the CURRENT scope list (which no longer contains that edge — a real disk-persisted state, not a hand-set object), and the real ScopeContextProvider fallback resolves it safely to the default scope with no throw before the support.hub pointer is followed and resolves to supporter-hub. The prior version's scope list still contained the person scope, so it exercised an ordinary transition, not a stale edge.

Dispatch-level mutual-exclusivity check (scope-context mocked, per that file's existing gc1-allow note): a Me-scope render taken independently of a Support-hub render shows no Support-hub content. Supplementary coverage; superseded as primary AC-3 evidence by the real-mechanism test below.

[rework, WI-2223 rejection fix] Real-mechanism return-path test (new co-located file; ScopeContextProvider UNMOCKED): drives the real support.hub push (real pushNowDeepLink + real setActiveScope) from Me to Support-hub, then drives the real 'return to Me' switch via the same setActiveScope the ScopeChip (_layout.tsx) calls, and asserts the Me surface renders with zero Support-hub content. scope-context.tsx has no navigation-event listener (no useFocusEffect/blur handler) and ScopeContextProvider mounts above the Tabs navigator at _layout.tsx root, so activeScope is structurally unaffected by back/pop navigation — 'return to Me' is this explicit scope switch, not a router.back() consequence, which is why this test drives that mechanism instead of a literal back(). The prior version did two independent renders with a hand-swapped mocked activeScope and never drove a transition.

[rework, evidenced infeasibility, not a deferral] The AC's cited nav-shell.spec.ts e2e fallback for the visible-layout claim is structurally unusable: nav-shell.spec.ts:40 and nav-shell.spec.ts:59 assert tab-mentor is NOT visible under the Config-F flag configuration that file tests, and the whole file is excluded from the default Playwright run by playwright.config.ts:216's testIgnore (config-f is opt-in only via the config-f-smoke project). A Support-hub-Mentor-surface case cannot exist there. The real-mechanism jest test above (real testID presence/absence on real rendered components) is treated as satisfying the visible-layout portion instead.
Test against the real ScopeContextProvider proves a learner-shape (me-only) account is unaffected: setActiveScope early-returns (activeScope stays 'me') while navigation still proceeds unchanged.
